# Organization GitHub Standards

This repository defines **organization-wide GitHub standards** for issue intake, pull requests, and contribution hygiene.

The contents of this repository are automatically applied to all repositories in the organization unless explicitly overridden.

---

## Purpose

The goal of these standards is to ensure that:
- Work items are clearly scoped
- Decisions are traceable
- Vendors and internal contributors follow the same intake rules
- Technical execution aligns with product intent

This repository is **governance infrastructure**, not an application codebase.

---

## Issue Templates

All repositories use standardized GitHub Issue Forms to ensure that every work item includes:
- Clear context and purpose
- Acceptance criteria
- Known risks or dependencies

Issues without acceptance criteria may be rejected or deferred.

Blank issues are disabled by default.

---

## Pull Requests

Where applicable, pull request templates guide contributors to:
- Explain what changed and why
- Link related issues
- Highlight risks or follow-up work

---

## Scope

This repository:
- Applies to all repositories in the organization
- Defines *how work is proposed and reviewed*
- Does not define product scope or priorities

Product planning, roadmaps, and specifications live in their respective repositories or shared documentation spaces.

---

## Enforcement

These standards are enforced through:
- GitHub configuration
- Review process
- Project governance policies

Exceptions must be documented and agreed upon.

---

## Questions

If you are unsure which template to use or how to structure an issue, contact the project owner or technical lead before creating new work items.
